title: Treasury Moves to Prevent Abuse of Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFI) Fund Programs
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury announced on April 9, 2026 that the CDFI Fund will issue rules requiring CDFI award recipients to comply with the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (PRWORA), effectively excluding illegal aliens and non-qualified aliens from receiving these federal benefits. The announcement also includes new anti-discrimination compliance provisions requiring certified CDFIs to adopt policies ensuring they do not engage in discrimination inconsistent with federal law.
key_points: Treasury will issue a notice of proposed rulemaking clarifying that CDFI Fund awards constitute 'federal public benefits' under PRWORA
Non-qualified aliens including illegal aliens would not be eligible to receive CDFI Fund benefits under the new rules
New provisions added to CDFI Fund agreements require certified CDFIs to comply with federal anti-discrimination laws
Certified CDFIs must adopt, implement, and maintain compliance policies, certify annually, and make them available for CDFI Fund review
Non-compliance remedies include potential decertification, termination of unused funds, and recapture of past award funds
Announcement attributed to Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent
why_it_matters: This is a Treasury administrative/regulatory announcement affecting Community Development Financial Institutions. It establishes new compliance requirements for CDFI certification and award eligibility, including PRWORA eligibility verification and anti-discrimination provisions. The changes primarily impact organizations receiving CDFI Fund grants and do not directly affect broad financial markets, interest rates, or monetary policy.
